title: lithocarpus propinquus
intro: Lithocarpus propinquus, also known as the tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family native to the mountainous regions of Taiwan. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Lithocarpus propinquus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate cough, and soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into a tea, or used in tincture form to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
